pattern: Repeatedly dividing by three reduces any power of three to one with no remainder, so the test is whether the value is positive and only ever divisible by three down to one.
hints: A power of three is positive, and dividing it by three leaves no remainder until you reach one.
Keep dividing the number by three while it stays evenly divisible; a true power of three will land exactly on one.
Reject anything not positive, then loop while the value is divisible by three dividing it down; return whether you finished at exactly one.

edge_inputs.description: negative input is never a power
one is three to the zero
a multiple of three that is not a pure power
just below the largest power of three
a non-power that is divisible by three twice
maximum signed int, not a power

stress.description: true powers of three interleaved with nearby non-powers
stress.note: mixes exact powers, off-by-one neighbours, and random integers
